steps.title: Identify what the blank must mean
steps.content: The second sentence begins with "Yet" and says Hale's parents would have been "correct" if they had made a prediction when he was born. This means the first sentence is describing something parents rarely do: predict their child's career. The blank must be a word meaning to predict or expect.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — disagree: "Few new parents disagree that their baby will become an astronaut" implies most parents think it will happen, which contradicts the rarity implied by the passage. ✗
  • Choice B — ignore: "Ignore" means to disregard, not to predict or expect. The sentence is about forming a prediction, not about dismissing something. ✗
  • Choice C — require: "Require" means to demand or need. Parents requiring their baby to become an astronaut makes no logical sense in this context. ✗
  • Choice D — anticipate: "Anticipate" means to predict or expect in advance. "Few new parents anticipate their baby becoming an astronaut" directly sets up the contrast: Hale's parents would have been correct to make exactly that prediction. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
